message:
fix(ephemeral): resolve symlinks before ephemeral-root classification

Both `classify_ephemeral` and `path_has_ephemeral_root` previously
checked the raw (literal) path against system temp prefixes. A project
root like `/data/projects/foo` that is a symlink to `/tmp/test-foo/`
would be classified as Production instead of Ephemeral, bypassing the
storage isolation reroute and allowing ephemeral data to land in the
shared global mailbox.

Now both functions call `std::fs::canonicalize()` before the Tier 1
path checks. If canonicalization fails (broken symlink, permission
error), the raw path is used as a safe fallback — this preserves the
previous behavior for non-symlink paths while closing the symlink gap.

Found during fifth-pass code review of the durability program work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
